csv_description: This variable designates which tokamak has supplied the data.
The date of the most recent update for any variable listed in the database. The format is YYYYMMDD (Year-Month-Day).
transform_args: (lambda s: f'{("" if len(s)==8 else ("19" if int(s[:2])>=70 else "20"))+s[:-4]}-{s[-4:-2]}-{s[-2:]}T00:00:00Z')(str(int(LCUPDATE)).zfill(6))

csv_description: The date the shot was taken. The format is YYYYMMDD (Year-Month-Day).
The shot from which the data are taken.
Time during the shot at which the data are taken in seconds.
Start of main time average window in seconds.
The ELM frequency in Hertz. JT60U: Average over 400-500 msec.

Effective atomic mass in AMU. Based on concentration measurements in JET, START and TFTR or calculated as = 0.5 (PGASA + 0.5 (BGASA + BGASA2)) if PINJ > 0 and PINJ2 > 0. = 0.5 (PGASA + BGASA) if only PINJ > 0. = PGASA otherwise. JET: A few Ohmic observations PNBI <= 3 kW. For these observations MEFF = PGASA.
notes: IMAS definition is explicit about hydrogenic species. However PGASA and MEFF in database include He3 and He4 as the "working gas".

csv_description: Mass number of the neutral beam gas (real value). Possible values are: 0 When PINJ = 0 1 Hydrogen 2 Deuterium 3 3He or Tritium 4 4He
Charge number of the neutral beam gas (integer value). Possible values are: 0 When PINJ = 0 1 Hydrogen, Deuterium or Tritium 2 Helium
Mass number of the second neutral beam gas (real value). Possible values are: 0 When PINJ2 = 0 1 Hydrogen 2 Deuterium 3 3He or Tritium 4 4He
Charge number of the second neutral beam gas (integer value). Possible values are: 0 When PINJ2 = 0 1 Hydrogen, Deuterium or Tritium 2 Helium
The plasma geometrical major radius in meters, from an MHD equilibrium fit, defined as the average of the minimum and the maximum radial extent of the plasma.
The major radius of the magnetic axis in meters from an MHD equilibrium fit. ASDEX: Formula based on a number of equilibriums.
The horizontal plasma minor radius in meters from an MHD equilibrium fit. ASDEX: Formula based on a number of equilibriums.
The plasma elongation determined from an MHD equilibrium fit. ASDEX: Formula based on a number of equilibriums. PDX: k = 1 for all records.
notes: Also has a corresponding leaf in equilibrium

csv_description: Upper triangularity of the plasma boundary from an MHD equilibrium fit.
Lower triangularity of the plasma boundary from an MHD equilibrium fit.
The plasmas volume in m3 determined from an MHD equilibrium fit. ASDEX: Formula based on a number of equilibriums.
The minimum distance between the separatrix flux surface and either the vessel wall or limiters in meters from an MHD equilibrium fit. ASDEX: Formula based on a number of equilibriums.
The material of the vessel wall. Possible values are: C Carbon SS for Stainless Steel IN for Inconel IN/C for Inconel with Carbon CSS for (partly) Carbon on Stainless Steel. AL for Aluminum
The material of the limiters. Possible values are: C for Carbon BE for Beryllium MO for Molybdenum NONE for no Limiter
notes: dictionary needs to be determined

csv_description: The evaporated material used to cover the inside of the vessel. Possible values are: BOR BO for Boron BOROA for Boron (B2H6 + CH4 + H2) BOROB for Boron (B2H6 + H2) BOROC for Boron BOROX for Boron B2D6 for Boron CARB for Carbon CARBH for Carbon (CH4 + D2) CARBORANEfor Orto-Carborane (C2B10H12) into He Glow DECABORA ??? TI for Titanium BE for Beryllium SILICON for Silicon SID4 for Silicon NONE for no evaporation JET to repair
notes: dictionary needed since "NONE" is in data as string
source: BOROA = (B2H6 + CH4 + H2), BOROB = (B2H6 + H2), BOROC = BORO = BOR = BO = B = Boron

csv_description: The vacuum Toroidal magnetic field in Tesla at RGEO determined from the TF coil current. AUG/CMOD/MAST: Normally negative BT values. JET: Negative BT values indicate reversed Toroidal field.

The plasma current in Amperes determined from an external Rogowski loop with vessel current subtraction. CMOD/JET/TDEV: Normally negative IP values. Positive IP values indicate operation with reversed current.
The loop voltage at the plasma boundary in volts.

The plasma safety factor from an MHD equilibrium fit evaluated at the flux surface that encloses 95% of the total poloidal flux. ASDEX: Q95 = qcyl (1 + (AMIN/RGEO)^2 (1 + 0.5 BEILI2^2)) with qcyl = 5e6 (BT/IP) (AMIN^2/RGEO) (1+KAPPA^2)/2. TFTR: Q at boundary defined by limiter.
Poloidal beta computed from the MHD equilibrium fit. ASDEX: BEPMHD equals BEIMHD. TFTR: BEPMHD equals BEILI2 - li/2 with li/2 from time evolution model.
notes: BEIMHD and BEPMHD both map here; verify which definition applies.

csv_description: Toroidal beta computed from the MHD equilibrium fit. TFTR: Computed from BEPMHD.

Central line average electron density in m-3 from interferometer or LIDAR (JET). JET: NEL has been approximated if no measurement is available: Ohmic NEL ~ exp{2.931 + 0.873 ln(NEV) + 0.064 ln(NE0)}, H-mode NEL ~ exp{3.745 + 0.825 ln(NEV) + 0.092 ln(NE0)}. See NELFORM.
The time rate of change of NEL in m-3/s.

The volume averaged electron density in m-3. ASDEX: From 4 HCN interferometer channels. D3D: Spline fit to CO2 and Thomson scattering data. JET: Weighted summation over 6 interferometer channels. JFT2M: Analytic fit with fixed profile shape to 2 channels.
The central electron density at the magnetic axis in m-3, determined in same manner as NEV.
Line average plasma effective charge determined from visible Bremsstrahlung.
Total radiated power in watts as measured by Bolometer.

Total Ohmic power in watts. ASDEX: max{0, VSURF x IP}. AUG: VSURF x IP (VSURF corrected for flux variations). CMOD: Vres x IP (Vres corrected for inductive effects). D3D: CxB10x Ip^2 RGEO^2/(WT ne). JET: Corrected for inductance effects. JT60U SHOT<33635: VSURF x IP; SHOT>=33635: Te profile with TOPICS code. TFTR: POHM = IP x VSURF - d(3.14e-7 RGEO x IP^2 x li)/dt.
The injected neutral beam power (beam species BGASA/BGASZ) that passes into the torus in watts. Zero if no beams on. Total NBI power = PINJ + PINJ2.
The injected neutral beam power from a second source (species BGASA2/BGASZ2) in watts. Zero if no second-source beams on.
Fraction of beam power co-injected as compared to the total beam power injected.
Total injected neutral beam power minus shine through in watts. Zero if no beams on.
notes: HIGH CONFIDENCE: summary/heating_current_drive/power_nbi is 'Total auxiliary power coupled from NBI into the plasma', matching PNBI = total injected NBI minus shine-through losses.

csv_description: ECRH frequency in Hz. Zero if no ECRH is applied.

Mode of ECRH waves. Possible values are: NONE If no ECRH power has been applied to discharge OFF If PECRH = 0 but PECRH > 0 at some other time in discharge O is ordinary mode X is extraordinary X+O both ordinary and extraordinary mode UNKNOWNMode of ECRH unknown
Frequency of ICRH waves in Hz. Zero, if no ICRH is applied.

Time rate of change of the total plasma stored energy as determined by the diamagnetic loop in watts. ASDEX: Parabolic fit over ±6 ms; set by hand when DWHC=1. D3D: (±25%). JET: Running average over ±100 ms. JFT2M: Simple derivative over ±5 ms.
notes: DD path confirmed: summary/global_quantities/denergy_diamagnetic_dt (W) = 'Time derivative of the diamagnetic plasma energy content'. The CSV description says 'as determined by the diamagnetic loop' – this refers to the measurement instrument, not a different quantity: the diamagnetic loop IS the diagnostic that measures d(Wdia)/dt. Suggest mapped.

csv_description: The volume averaged electron temperature in eV. ASDEX: From 16 radial YAG measurements fitting Te(x) = Te(0) exp(ax2+bx4+cx6). D3D: Spline fit to Thomson scattering data. JET: From 51-point ECE profile. PBXM/PDX: Computed from BETMHD, VOL, NEL assuming Zeff=1.
notes: units???

csv_description: The electron temperature at the magnetic axis in eV. ASDEX: From 16 radial YAG measurements. D3D: Spline fit to Thomson scattering. JET: From ECE profile.
notes: units???

csv_description: The volume averaged ion temperature in eV. D3D: Spline fit to charge exchange recombination data. JET: Estimated from TIV = TI0 x TEV/TE0. PBXM/PDX: TIV = TEV.
notes: units???

csv_description: Total plasma energy in joules as determined from the diamagnetic loop. ASDEX: In ohmic WDIA = Wdia-meas where Wdia-meas = 0.471 x RGEO x 10-6 x IP2 x BEPDIA; in H-mode WDIA = Wdia-meas - DW. JFT2M: In ohmic WDIA = Wdia-meas; in H-mode WDIA = Wdia-meas - DW. TFTR: WDIA = 4.71 x 10-7 RGEO x IP2 x BEPDIA.

Total plasma energy in joules as determined by a MHD equilibrium fit or based on probe measurements and an estimate of li/2 (ASDEX). ASDEX: 0.471 x RGEO x 10-6 x IP2 x BEPMHD. TFTR: WMHD = WTOT currently.

Total thermal plasma energy in joules as determined from kinetic measurements. ASDEX: WEKIN (1 + (7-ZEFF)/(7-1)). JET: From profile fit assuming flat Zeff and Ti profile as Te. TEXTOR: WDIA - 1.5 WFPER.
Total thermal electron plasma energy in joules as determined from kinetic measurements.
Total thermal ion plasma energy in joules as determined from kinetic measurements. JET: From ECE temperature profile shape normalized to Ti(0) from crystal X-ray diagnostic.
Total parallel fast ion energy due to NBI in joules from transport calculations. Zero if no NBI. JET: PENCIL code. JFT2M: Monte Carlo. PBXM/PDX/TEXTOR: TRANSP.
notes: conflict with WFICRH (127)

csv_description: Estimated total plasma energy content in Joules. Machine-specific definition: ASDEX: WTH+WFFORM; AUG/CMOD/D3D/JT60U/MAST/START/TCV: WMHD; COMPASS: WDIA; JET: WTH+WFPER+WFPAR+WFICRH; JFT2M: WTH+WFFORM; NSTX: WTH; PBXM/PDX: WTH+WFPER+WFPAR; TDEV: WKIN; TEXTOR/TFTR: as defined; TUMAN3M/T10: WDIA.
Estimated loss power corrected for charge exchange and unconfined orbit losses in watts. Defined as PLTH = PL - PFLOSS.
Estimated thermal energy confinement time in seconds. Defined as WTH/PLTH.
notes: Thermal energy confinement time = WTH/PLTH. Same tau_energy conflict as TAUTOT. Candidate note only: summary/global_quantities/tau_energy with mapped_caveat. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Type of ELMs. Possible values are: NONE No ELMs TYPE-I Type I ELMs TYPE-II Type II ELMs ( no data) TYPE-III Type III ELMs TYPE-V Type V ELMs TYPE-1+2 Mixed Type I and Type II ELMs TYPE-1+5 Mixed Type I and Type V ELMs TYPE-RF ICRH ELMs on JET UNKNOWNType has not been determined
notes: field is int_1D… No way to express mixed type ELMS. Dictionary incomplete

csv_description: Charge number of the plasma working gas (integer value). Possible values are: 1 Hydrogen, Deuterium or Tritium 2 Helium 7 Nitrogen
notes: "working gas"?

csv_description: The plasma configuration. Possible values are: SN Single null SN(L) Single null with the x-point at the bottom of the machine SN(U) Single null with the x-point at the top of the machine DN Double null DND Double null divertor IW Inner wall. LIM Outboard limiter BOT Limited on bottom of vessel TOP Limited on top of vessel MAR Marginally diverted/limited Tokamak details: TOK SN SN(L) SN(U) DN DND IW LIM BOT TOP MAR ASDEX x x AUG x CMOD x x x COMPASS x D3D x x x x x x x x JET x x x x JFT2M x x JT60U x PBXM x PDX x START x T10 x TCV x x TDEV x TEXTOR x TFTR x TUMAN3M x MAST x NSTX x ASDEX: DN if vertical shift DZ is less than 5 mm, otherwise SN. D3D: DN if two nulls and the separatrix flux surface are inside the divertor tiles and on the same flux surface within 0.25 cm. JFT2M: DN if two nulls are inside the limiter.
notes: is MAR = snowflake? In addition, definitions of IN, OUT are not specified in variable definitions
source: LIM = 0, SN = 1, SN(L) = 11, SNL = 11, SNB = 11 SN(U) = 12, SNT = 12, DN = 13

csv_description: The magnetic shear at the flux surface that encloses 95% of the total poloidal flux.
notes: TBD: Perhaps profiles1d in equilbrium is the more appropriate spot!

csv_description: Poloidal diamagnetic beta. ASDEX: Corrected poloidal diamagnetic beta using an average of the 3 Ohmic points in the database.
notes: beta_pol is the only non-MHD poloidal beta in summary, but its DD definition is equilibrium-pressure based, not diamagnetic-specific - no dedicated diamagnetic beta field exists.

csv_description: ECRH power in watts coupled to the plasma. Zero, if no ECRH is applied.
notes: ECRH power coupled to plasma. Candidate: summary/heating_current_drive/ec(0)/power_launched (launched from antenna ≈ coupled in most ECRH cases). Caveat: IMAS 'power_launched' = from launcher into vessel; 'coupled to plasma' in DB4v5 may be slightly less (reflection losses). For ECRH these are generally equated. Also candidate: summary/heating_current_drive/power_launched_ec for total.

csv_description: Antenna phasing. Possible Values are: NONE If no ICRH power has been applied to discharge OFF If PICRH = 0 but PICRH > 0 at some other time in discharge DIPOLE Dipole phasing MONOPOLEMonopole phasing UNKNOWN Antenna phasing not supplied
notes: target path is a radian float_1D time coordinated. Possible values of source csv is MONOPOLE, DIPOLE, 0PI0PI. The last cannot be expressed as a single number?

csv_description: ICRH power in watts coupled to the plasma. Zero, if no ICRH is applied.
notes: ICRH power coupled to plasma. Candidate: summary/heating_current_drive/ic(0)/power_launched (launched = coupled for ICRH). Analogous to PECRHC for ECRH. Same launched-vs-coupled ambiguity; ICRH reflection losses can be significant.

csv_description: The ion temperature at the magnetic axis in eV. D3D: Spline fit to charge exchange recombination data. JET: From Crystal X-ray diagnostic.
notes: units???

csv_description: Total perpendicular fast ion energy due to NBI in joules from transport calculations. Zero if no NBI. JET: PENCIL code. JFT2M: Monte Carlo. PBXM/PDX/TEXTOR: TRANSP.
Describes each device's divertor configuration TOK

Type of auxiliary heating. Possible values are: NONE No Auxiliary heating NB Neutral Beam Injection IC Ion Cyclotron Resonance Heating EC Electron Cyclotron Resonance Heating ECOA Electron Cyclotron Resonance Heating ( Off-axis ) ECIC Combined ECRH + ICRH NBEC Combined NBI + ECRH NBIC Combined NBI + ICRH NBECICCombined NBI + ECRH + ICRH
notes: Not made redundant by corresponding power column

csv_description: The phase of the discharge at TIME. Possible values are: OHM Ohmic L L-mode RI Radiative I-mode LHLHL H-mode with frequent LH transitions H ELM-free H-mode HSELM H-mode with small ELMs HSELMH H-mode with high frequency small ELMs HGELM H-mode with large ELMs HGELMH H-mode with high frequency large ELMs H??? ELM-free H-mode to be confirmed HGELM???H-mode with large ELMs to be confirmed
The time of the L to H transition in seconds.
notes: Important quantity. Potential discussion for summary IDS. Event name and event time , list of events.

csv_description: The triangularity of the plasma boundary from an MHD equilibrium fit.
Area of plasma cross section in m2 determined from an MHD equilibrium fit. ASDEX: Formula based on a number of equilibriums.
notes: "Area of plasma cross section" = LCFS poloidal cross section?

csv_description: The material of the divertor tiles. Possible values are: SS for Stainless Steel C CC for Carbon TI1 TI2 for Titanium, BE for Beryllium C/BE for Carbon at the top and Beryllium at the bottom W for Wolfram MO for Molybdenum IN for Inconel NONE for no Divertor
notes: dictionary needed for consistency with 2008 crosswalk

csv_description: Indicates, when CONFIG = SN, whether the ion grad-B drift is towards ( = 1) or pointing away from ( = -1) the X-point.
notes: Important quantity. Potentially derived, check with TC26 if CONFIG and sign of BT have a certain relationship.

csv_description: Quantity bI + 0.5 li, where bI is the Shafranov beta and li the internal inductance, determined from MHD or probe measurements (ASDEX).
notes: Composite bI + li/2 has no DD field; the components exist separately (summary/global_quantities/beta_pol, summary/global_quantities/li). Suggest derived or temporary.

csv_description: Beta Shafranov from MHD. ASDEX: BEIMHD equals BEILI2 minus an estimate of li/2 obtained during the Ohmic phase assuming a resistive equilibrium. TFTR: BEIMHD equals (2xBEPMHD + BEPDIA)/3.
Electron density at the separatrix in m-3.
notes: Electron density at the separatrix. Path confirmed by DD lookup: summary/local/separatrix/n_e is a structure node (needs_source=TRUE set; value leaf is FLT_1D dynamic). Only AUG and JET have data in DB5.2.3; specific definitions and error values not specified in PDF for either device. Verify source string before promoting to mapped.

csv_description: Electron density in the scrape-off layer in m-3.
notes: Electron density in the scrape-off layer. No confirmed summary-level scalar path in the DD. No summary/local/sol/n_e node found. Possible edge_profiles IDS route but no direct scalar. Only AUG and JET have data in DB5.2.3; error values not specified in PDF. Suggest temporary until an appropriate DD path is identified.

csv_description: Neutral pressure in the main chamber in Pa.
notes: Neutral pressure in main chamber (Pa). Candidate: barometry/gauge(:)/pressure for a main-chamber-tagged gauge in the barometry IDS. No summary-level scalar path. Machine-specific gauge assignment required. Only AUG and JET have data in DB5.2.3; error values not specified. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Neutral pressure in the divertor in Pa.
notes: Neutral pressure in the divertor (Pa). Candidate: barometry/gauge(:)/pressure for a divertor-tagged gauge in the barometry IDS. No summary-level scalar path. Machine-specific gauge assignment required. Only AUG and JET have data in DB5.2.3; error values not specified. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Gas puff in the main chamber in e/s (electrons per second).
notes: Gas puff rate in the main chamber (e/s = electrons per second). Candidate: gas_injection/pipe(:)/valve(:)/flow_rate for a main-chamber injection pipe in the gas_injection IDS. No summary-level scalar. Machine-specific pipe/valve assignment required. Analogous to FUELRATE. Only AUG and JET have data in DB5.2.3; error values not specified. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Gas puff in the divertor in e/s (electrons per second).
notes: Gas puff rate in the divertor (e/s = electrons per second). Candidate: gas_injection/pipe(:)/valve(:)/flow_rate for a divertor injection pipe in the gas_injection IDS. No summary-level scalar. Machine-specific pipe/valve assignment required. Analogous to GP_MAIN. Only AUG and JET have data in DB5.2.3; error values not specified. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Time derivative for ASDEX of WDIA from a parabolic fit to the three available H-mode points. DWDIAPAR has been used in calculating TAUDIA.
Equal to 1 when DWDIA or DWMHD have been corrected by hand for ASDEX.
notes: ASDEX hand-correction flag, no DD equivalent; suggest temporary.

csv_description: Thermal energy confinement time in seconds. Defined as WKIN/(POHM + PNBI + PICRH + PECRH - DWMHD - PFLOSS). ASDEX: DWTOT = 2/3 DWMHD + 1/3 DWDIA used instead of DWMHD.
notes: Derived quantities, however some live in temporary.

csv_description: Thermal energy confinement time in seconds. Defined as (WMHD - WFFORM - 0.75 WFICRH)/(POHM + PNBI + PICRH + PECRH - DWMHD - PFLOSS). ASDEX: 1.5 WFANI x WFFORM and DWTOT used for WFFORM and DWMHD. PBXM/PDX: 3/4xWFPER + 3/2xWFPAR used instead of WFFORM.
notes: Derived quantities, however some live in temporary.

csv_description: Estimated loss power not corrected for charge exchange and unconfined orbit losses in watts. Machine-specific formula differs per tokamak (POHM + heating powers - dW/dt terms).
Standard dataset flag for DB5. Value 1: standard selection for DB5 as defined in [Verdoolaege et al., 2021]. Value 0: all other observations. Note: can only be non-zero for observations in DB5.
notes: Standard dataset flag for DB5 (Boolean). Reference: Verdoolaege et al., 2021. No IMAS DD physics equivalent — database metadata flag only. Suggest discard; retain in crosswalk for completeness if downstream scripts need it.

csv_description: Integer equivalent of TIME, defined as INT[1000 x ROUND{TIME, 0.001}] where INT is the integer part and ROUND rounds TIME to the 3rd decimal place. Except for TUMAN-3M where INT[100000 x ROUND{TIME, 0.00001}] is used.
End of main time average window in seconds.
Mass number of the plasma working gas (real value). Possible values are: 1 Hydrogen 2 Deuterium 2.5 Deuterium and Tritium 3 3He or Tritium 4 4He 14.01 Nitrogen
notes: "Working gas"? Technically derivable from MEFF and Atomic mass number of NB injectors.

csv_description: New plasma elongation definition (= AREA / ( p AMIN2) ) COMPASS: AREA is approximated by VOL / (2p RGEO).
notes: Elongation = AREA/(π·AMIN²). Derivable from mapped fields (AREA and AMIN). No dedicated DD field. Suggest derived.

csv_description: New plasma elongation definition (= VOL / (2p RGEO) / ( p AMIN2) )
notes: Elongation = VOL/(2π·RGEO)/(π·AMIN²). Derivable from mapped VOL, RGEO, AMIN. Suggest derived.

csv_description: Surface area of the plasma from formula.
notes: Formula-based plasma surface area (not from equilibrium code). Candidate: equilibrium/time_slice(0)/global_quantities/surface. Caveat: AREA is already mapped to equilibrium/time_slice(0)/global_quantities/area; SURFFORM uses an analytical formula instead. Suggest temporary until path distinction with AREA is resolved.

csv_description: Total diamagnetic energy confinement time in seconds. Defined as WDIA/(POHM + PNBI + PICRH + PECRH - DWDIA).
notes: Derived quantities, however some live in temporary.

csv_description: Total MHD energy confinement time in seconds. Defined as WMHD/(POHM + PNBI + PICRH + PECRH - DWMHD).
notes: Derived quantities, however some live in temporary.

csv_description: This integer variable designates which tokamak has supplied the data. Possible values are listed in table for TOK.
notes: The string variable TOK is adequate to identify the machine

csv_description: Time point indicator (ASDEX only). Possible values are: 0 Indicator not used 1-3 Ohmic points 4 L-mode 5-7 H-mode points up to the time at which DWDIA = 0 8-9 Extra H-mode points - (no data)
notes: ASDEX-only bookkeeping flag, no DD equivalent

csv_description: Parameter scan identifier
notes: Database bookkeeping (scan id), no DD equivalent; suggest temporary. | csv_description truncated from 3441 chars; full flag-code/lookup table in DB4v5 PDF entry 24.

csv_description: Ergodic magnetic field coil current in Amperes.
notes: review data for H-mode

csv_description: Indicates if startup was done with ( = YES) or without ( = NO) pre-magnetization current. JET: If PREMAG = NO for pre 94 shots then WMHD is missing.
notes: Pre-magnetization startup flag (YES/NO). No DD field. Suggest temporary or discard.

csv_description: Offset during the Ohmic phase between MHD and diamagnetic. ASDEX: Between diamagnetic beta poloidal and MHD beta poloidal. JFT2M: Between diamagnetic energy and MHD energy in Joule.
notes: machine specific.

csv_description: Indicates whether NEL is an indirect measurement. Value 1 = NEL was not directly measured (e.g. approximated by formula when interferometry unavailable); value 0 = direct measurement.
notes: Flag: NEL approximated by formula (=1) or direct measurement (=0). Database diagnostic-quality indicator; no IMAS DD equivalent. Suggest temporary or discard.

csv_description: The power fractions injected by neutral beam with the second source. JET 89-90 data: 781606 for 80kV D, 652114 for 140kV D, 990000 for 3He or 4He beams.
notes: Candidate: summary/heating_current_drive/nbi(1)/beam_power_fraction – same as BSOURCE for NBI unit 1.

csv_description: Estimated alpha heating power in Deuterium-Tritium plasmas in watts. JET: estimated as 1.601 x 10-19 x neutrons/s x 3.5 x 106 W. Set to zero, if less than 0.01 x 106 W.
notes: Variable is JET, TFTR only (i.e. excluside to D-T operations)

csv_description: The ion temperature at the magnetic axis in eV. JET: From charge exchange recombination spectroscopy.
notes: Conflicts with TI0 for imas_path. Which diagnostic is the most robust? Regardless, data only for JET and JT60U.

csv_description: Correction factor for thermal confinement time TAUTH (Kardaun et al., Plasma Phys. Control. Nucl. Fus. Res. 3 (1993) 251). ASDEX: 1/(1.5-0.1 bo-0.15 ca) for ELMy or 1/(1.2-0.1 bo-0.15 ca) ELM-free (bo=1 if EVAP=BOROA/BOROB; ca=1 if EVAP=CARBH). JET: 1/0.85 if 870101<=DATE<=871231. PDX: 1/((1/3)(DALFDV/DALFMP))^0.4 for ELMy. Else TAUC92=1.
Correction factor for thermal confinement time TAUTH (Schissel et al., EPS Vol. 17C Part I (1993) 103). ASDEX: Same formula as TAUC92. PDX: 1/((1/2)(DALFDV/DALFMP))^0.4 for ELMy. Else TAUC93=1.
Enhancement factor TAUTOT x TAUC92 / ITER89-P. Scaling reference: Yushmanov et al., Nuclear Fusion 30 (1990).
Enhancement factor TAUTH x TAUC92 / ITERL96-P. Scaling reference: Kaye et al., Nuclear Fusion 37 (1997) 1303.
Enhancement factor TAUTH x TAUC92 / ITERH93-P. Scaling reference: Schissel et al., EPS Vol. 17C Part I (1993) 103.
Enhancement factor TAUTH x TAUC92 / ITERH92Y. Scaling reference: Kardaun et al., Plasma Phys. Control. Nucl. Fus. Res. 3 (1993) 251.
Enhancement factor TAUTH x TAUC93 / EPS97(ELMy). Scaling reference: Cordey et al., Plasma Phys. Control. Fusion 39 (1997) B115.
Enhancement factor TAUTH x TAUC92 / IPB98(y). Scaling reference: ITER Physics Basis Ch.2, Nuclear Fusion 39 (1999) 2175.
Enhancement factor TAUTH x TAUC92 / IPB98(y,1). Scaling reference: ITER Physics Basis Ch.2, Nuclear Fusion 39 (1999) 2175.
Enhancement factor TAUTH x TAUC92 / IPB98(y,2). Scaling reference: ITER Physics Basis Ch.2, Nuclear Fusion 39 (1999) 2175.
Enhancement factor TAUTH x TAUC92 / IPB98(y,3). Scaling reference: ITER Physics Basis Ch.2, Nuclear Fusion 39 (1999) 2175.
Enhancement factor TAUTH x TAUC92 / IPB98(y,4). Scaling reference: ITER Physics Basis Ch.2, Nuclear Fusion 39 (1999) 2175.
Standard dataset flag. Can only be non-zero for observations in DB.1. Possible values: 1 = observation belonged to standard dataset of ITERH.DB1; 0 = all other observations.
notes: Standard dataset flag (DB.1 membership). Database selection/bookkeeping; no IMAS DD physics equivalent. Suggest temporary or discard.

csv_description: Flagging variable making connections to DB.1. Can only be non-zero for observations in DB.2. SELDB1 = sum a(I.n) x 10^(n-1), n=1-3. I.1: also in DB.1; I.2: in DB.1 and satisfied old standard selection; I.3: satisfies DB.1 standard selection now.
notes: Selection flag: summary/tag/name is a single string already claimed by SELEC2007, so it cannot hold further flags; suggest temporary like the other SELDB* rows.

csv_description: Flagging variable for standard selection in DB.2. SELDB2 = sum a(II.n) x 10^(n-1), n=1-10. Bit flags encode: H-mode criterion (II.1), NBI-only with H/D injection (II.2), missing confinement data (II.3), no pellets (II.4), weak dW/dt (II.5), radiation < 60% (II.6), and others. Full logic in DB4v5 PDF entry 164. [...]
notes: See SELDB1. | csv_description truncated from 3544 chars; full flag-code/lookup table in DB4v5 PDF entry 164.

csv_description: Flagging variable for extra selection criteria in DB.2. SELDB2X = sum a(II.n) x 10^(n-11), n=11-20. Bit flags: high compression ratio (II.11), no hot-ion H-modes (II.12), ELMs present (II.13), strong auxiliary heating (II.14), stationary density (II.15), strong dW/dt (II.16), low Q95 limit (II.17), no beryllisation (II.18), no boronisation (II.19), no carbonization (II.20). Full logic in DB4v5 PDF entry 165.
notes: Extra selection flag for DB.2. Database selection/bookkeeping; no IMAS DD equivalent. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Standard dataset flag. Can only be non-zero for observations in DB.2. Value 1: ELMy observations in ITERH92-P(y) subset (Kardaun et al., Plasma Phys. Control. Nucl. Fus. Res. 3 (1993) 251). Value 0: all other observations.
notes: Dataset flag: ITERH92-P(y) ELMy subset (Kardaun 1993). No IMAS DD equivalent. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Standard dataset flag. Can only be non-zero for observations in DB.3v5. Value 1: ELMy observations in subset DB2.5 (JET Report JET P(98)17 / ITER Physics Basis Ch.2, NF 39 (1999) 2175). Value 0: all other.
notes: Dataset flag: DB2.5 ELMy subset (ITER Physics Basis 1999). No IMAS DD equivalent. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Standard dataset flag. Can only be non-zero for observations in DB.3v5. Value 1: ELMy observations in subset DB2.8 (JET Report JET P(98)17 / ITER Physics Basis Ch.2, NF 39 (1999) 2175). Value 0: all other.
notes: Dataset flag: DB2.8 ELMy subset (ITER Physics Basis 1999). No IMAS DD equivalent. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Standard dataset flag. Can only be non-zero for observations in DB.3v5. Value 1: ELMy observations in subset DB3r(IS) (JET Report JET P(98)17 / ITER Physics Basis Ch.2, NF 39 (1999) 2175). Value 0: all other.
notes: Dataset flag: DB3r(IS) subset. No IMAS DD equivalent. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Standard dataset flag. Can only be non-zero for observations in DB.3v5. Value 1: standard dataset selection for DB3v5 (JET Report JET P(98)17 / ITER Physics Basis Ch.2, NF 39 (1999) 2175). Value 0: all other.
notes: Dataset flag: DB3v5 standard selection. No IMAS DD equivalent. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Standard dataset flag. See SELDB3X for selection details. Value 1: small IAEA2000 standard dataset (Kardaun et al., Fusion Energy 2000, IAEA Vienna 2001). Value 0: all other.
notes: Dataset flag: small IAEA2000 standard set (Kardaun 2001). No IMAS DD equivalent. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Standard dataset flag. See SELDB3X for selection details. Value 1: large IAEA2000 standard dataset (Kardaun et al., Fusion Energy 2000, IAEA Vienna 2001). Value 0: all other.
notes: Dataset flag: large IAEA2000 standard set (Kardaun 2001). No IMAS DD equivalent. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Standard dataset flag. See SELDB3X for selection details. Value 1: deuterium-only standard dataset (Kardaun et al., H-mode Workshop San Diego 2003). Value 0: all other.
notes: Dataset flag: H-mode Workshop 2003 deuterium-only subset. No IMAS DD equivalent. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Standard dataset flag. See SELDB3X for selection details. Value 1: IAEA2004 standard dataset (Cordey et al., Fusion Energy 2004, IAEA Vienna 2004). Value 0: all other.
notes: Dataset flag: IAEA2004 standard set (Cordey 2004). No IMAS DD equivalent. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Standard dataset flag. See SELDB3X for selection details. Value 1: ITER-like standard dataset (Cordey et al., Fusion Energy 2004, IAEA Vienna 2004). Value 0: all other.
notes: Dataset flag: IAEA2004 ITER-like subset. No IMAS DD equivalent. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Standard dataset flag. See SELDB3X for selection details. Value 1: deuterium-only standard dataset (McDonald et al., Nuclear Fusion). Value 0: all other.
notes: Dataset flag: deuterium-only subset (McDonald et al.). No IMAS DD equivalent. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Standard dataset flag. See SELDB3X for selection details. Value 1: standard dataset from 2005 H-mode Workshop (Kaye et al., St. Petersburg Sept 2005) for 1.6 <= kappa <= 2.43 and 1.6 <= MEFF <= 2.4, excluding TCV and JT60U. Includes 36 PBXM data points. Value 0: all other.
notes: Dataset flag: H-mode Workshop 2005 subset (Kaye et al.). No IMAS DD equivalent. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Standard dataset flag. Value 1: standard dataset as defined in Kardaun et al. (2006). Value 0: all other observations.
notes: Dataset flag: Kardaun et al. 2006 subset. No IMAS DD equivalent. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Flagging variable for standard selection in DB.3. SELDB3 = sum a(III.n) x 10^(n-1), n=1-10. Bit flags: H-mode criterion (III.1), no missing confinement data (III.2), no pellets (III.3), weak dW/dt (III.4), radiation < 60% (III.5), and others. Full logic (including tokamak-specific exceptions) in DB4v5 PDF entry 179. [...]
notes: Dataset selection flag for DB.3 standard set. No IMAS DD equivalent. Suggest temporary. (Description truncated; full logic in DB4v5 PDF entry 179.)

csv_description: Flagging variable for extra selection criteria in DB.3. SELDB3X = sum a(III.n) x 10^(n-11), n=11-16. Bit flags: withdrawn data (III.11), strong gas puff (III.12), Ti0/Te0 ratio limit (III.13), internal inductance limit (III.14), large/small IAEA2000 equivalent datasets (III.15/16). Full logic in DB4v5 PDF entry 180. [...]
notes: Dataset extra-selection flag for DB.3. No IMAS DD equivalent. Suggest temporary. (Description truncated; full logic in DB4v5 PDF entry 180.)

csv_description: Standard dataset flag for DB3. Value 1: standard selection for DB3 as defined in [ITER Physics Basis, Nucl. Fusion 39 (1999) 2175]. Value 0: all other observations.
notes: Standard dataset flag for DB3 (Boolean). No IMAS DD physics equivalent — database metadata flag only. Suggest discard; retain in crosswalk for completeness if downstream scripts need it.

csv_description: Flagging variable for new standard selection. SELDB4 = sum a(IV.n) x 10^(n-1), n=1-10. IV.1 Hybrid H-mode criterion: a(IV.1) = 1 if HYBRID = YES, HYBRID or IH.
notes: Dataset selection flag for DB.4 standard set (hybrid H-mode criterion). No IMAS DD equivalent. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Standard dataset flag for DB4v5. Value 1: standard selection for DB4v5. Value 0: all other observations. Note: can only be non-zero for observations in DB4v5.
notes: Standard dataset flag for DB4v5 (Boolean). No IMAS DD physics equivalent — database metadata flag only. Suggest discard; retain in crosswalk for completeness if downstream scripts need it.

csv_description: Hybrid discharge indicator . Possible values are: NO Not a Hybrid discharge YES Hybrid discharge IH Improved H-mode (AUG) HYBRID Hybrid H-mode (DIII-D) UNKNOWNNot checked for HYBRID properties
notes: Hybrid/Improved H-mode discharge indicator string. No dedicated summary flag field in DD. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Internal Transport Barrier indicator. Possible values are: NOITB No ITB's - (no data) PREITB Before ITB onset - (no data) ITB ITB present - (no data) UNKNOWNNot checked for ITB
notes: Internal Transport Barrier presence indicator. summary/local/itb* nodes exist for profile quantities but no Boolean ITB-presence flag. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Type of Internal Transport Barrier. Possible values are: NONE No ITB - (no data) TI Ti ITB - (no data) TE Te ITB - (no data) NE ne ITB - (no data) TITENEITB's in Te, Ti and ne - (no data) NA Not applicable
notes: ITB type string (Ti/Te/ne/combined). No DD field. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: The average ELM amplitude of the H-alpha signal (minus base level) in arbitrary units.
notes: Average ELM H-alpha amplitude (arbitrary units). No IMAS path; diagnostic and machine-specific H-alpha signal. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: The average ELM duration of the H-alpha signal in seconds.
notes: Average ELM duration (H-alpha signal width). No IMAS path. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: The average ELM integral of the H-alpha signal (minus base level) in arbitrary units.
notes: Average ELM H-alpha integral. No IMAS path. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: The time of the Ohmic to L-mode transition (start of auxiliary heating) in seconds.
notes: Ohmic to L-mode transition time. No standard IMAS path for transition-event timestamps. Candidate: summary IDS could hold tagged events, but no specific field found. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Pellet material if a pellet(s) has been injected. Possible values are: NONE No pellets H Hydrogen pellet(s) D Deuterium pellet(s) GP_H Strong Hydrogen gas fuelling GP_D Strong Deuterium gas fuelling GAS- FUEL Strong gas fuelling (JET)
notes: Pellet material string. Candidate: pellets/time_slice/pellet/species (pellets IDS) — not a summary scalar. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Fuel rate in electrons per second of extra gas.
notes: Fuel rate from extra gas injection (e/s). Candidate: gas_injection/pipe/valve/flow_rate (gas_injection IDS) — not in summary. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Atomic Charge of extra fuelled gas. Possible values are: 0 No extra fuelled gas 1 Hydrogen 2 Helium 7 Nitrogen 10 Neon 16 Sulfur - should this be 14 for Silicon? 18 Argon 36 Krypton
notes: Atomic charge of extra fuelled gas. Candidate: gas_injection/pipe/valve/species/z (gas_injection IDS). No summary path. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Atomic Mass of extra fuelled gas. Possible values are: 0 No extra fuelled gas 1 Hydrogen 2 Deuterium 3 Tritium / Helium 4 Helium ~ 14 Nitrogen ~ 20.18 Neon 28 Silicon ~ 39.95 Argon 83.8 Krypton
notes: Atomic mass of extra fuelled gas. Candidate: gas_injection/pipe/valve/species/a. No summary path. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Indentation of the plasma determined from an MHD equilibrium fit.
notes: No indentation field in the current DD (boundary carries squareness, elongation, triangularity); suggest temporary.

csv_description: The minimum distance between the X-point and either the vessel walls or limiters in meters from an MHD equilibrium fit. The value is positive if X-point is inside either the vessel wall or limiters.
notes: X-point to vessel-wall/limiter distance. summary/boundary/gap_limiter_wall is SEPLIM (separatrix-limiter). No dedicated X-point-to-wall field in summary. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Da emission on the midplane.
notes: No summary-level D-alpha scalar. Nearest diagnostic path: spectrometer_visible/channel(0)/grating_spectrometer/processed_line(0)/intensity/data for a midplane spectroscopy channel. Suggest temporary or mapped_caveat at diagnostic level with channel-index caveat.

csv_description: Da emission in the divertor.
notes: Dα emission in the divertor (arbitrary units). No summary scalar. Nearest: spectrometer_visible/channel/.../processed_line/intensity for a divertor channel. Machine-specific channel assignment required. Suggest temporary (analogous to DALFMP).

csv_description: Current relaxation time in seconds from D.R. Mikkelsen Phys. Fluids B1 (1989) 333. TAUCR = 2.51e-6 x IP x RGEO / VSURF
notes: Current relaxation time: TAUCR = 2.51e-6 × IP × RGEO / VSURF. Fully derivable from mapped fields (IP, RGEO, VSURF). Suggest derived.

csv_description: Bootstrap current fraction.
notes: Bootstrap current fraction (Ibs/Ip). Candidate: summary/global_quantities/current_bootstrap is total bootstrap current in A, not a fraction. No fraction field found in summary. Suggest temporary or derived (current_bootstrap / global_quantities/ip).

csv_description: Normalized radius of q = 2 surface.
notes: Normalized radius of q=2 surface. Derivable from equilibrium/time_slice/profiles_1d/q profile. No scalar global q=2 radius field in summary. Suggest derived or temporary.

csv_description: Normalized sawtooth inversion radius as estimated by eq.(7) in H. Weisen et al, Nuclear Fusion 42 (2002) 136. RHOINV = 4e-7 x IP x RGEO / (AMIN^2 x BT x KAPPA x (KAPPA + 1/KAPPA))
notes: Normalized sawtooth inversion radius (Weisen formula). Candidate: sawteeth IDS may hold inversion radius profiles. In summary no direct scalar path. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: The electron density in m-3 determined from Thomson scattering. ASDEX: Average of the 3 YAG laser channels closest to the equatorial plane. D3D: Thomson scattering point closest to the magnetic axis (< 10 cm).
notes: Central electron density from Thomson scattering specifically. Candidate: same as NE0 → summary/local/magnetic_axis/n_e with needs_source='Thomson scattering'. Caveat: NE0 is already mapped to that path via profile fit; NE0TSC and NE0 may differ when the profile fit diverges from the TS point. Suggest temporary or mapped_caveat with needs_source to distinguish.

csv_description: Plasma effective charge as determined by neo-classical resistivity. ASDEX: Determined such that the current profile calculated from Zeff-neo, Te(r) and Uloop is consistent with measured total Ip. JET: Determined using volume averaged quantities.
notes: Zeff from neoclassical resistivity method (vs Bremsstrahlung for ZEFF). Candidate: summary/volume_average/zeff (if neoclassical Zeff is effectively volume-averaged). Distinguish from ZEFF (line-average/zeff) using needs_source. Suggest temporary until source distinction is resolved.

csv_description: Neutral beam energy weighted by power in eV. Calculated as Sum(Ei x Pi)/Sum(Pi) where Ei and Pi are beam energy and power for source i. ASDEX: Primary energy component given.
notes: ENBI is power-weighted across all NBI units; nbi(0)/energy is unit 0 only. Accept mapped_caveat for single-unit shots; multi-unit: consider temporary or formula.

csv_description: The power fractions injected by neutral beam. For example if P1 = 80%, P2 = 10% and P3 = 10% then BSOURCE = 801010.
notes: Candidate: summary/heating_current_drive/nbi(0)/beam_power_fraction (3-element array: full/half/third-energy fractions). Matches if BSOURCE encodes energy-component split of one beam. If it is fractional power of unit 0 vs total, no DD field exists → temporary.

csv_description: Amount of neutral beam power in watts lost from the plasma through charge exchange and unconfined orbits. ASDEX: From FREYA code fits. D3D: PNBI x exp{3.3 - 2.5 IP/1e6}/100. JET: PINJ x exp{3.35 - 0.667|IP|/1e6 - 0.2 NEL/1e19}/100.
notes: No summary-level field for CX + orbit losses. Closest: distributions/.../power_first_orbit (orbit only) or shinethrough/power (not CX). Derivable as PABS - power_nbi. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Location of ECRH launch. Possible values are: NONE If no ECRH power has been applied to discharge OFF If PECRH = 0 but PECRH > 0 at some other time in discharge IN identifies waves launched from the high field side OUT is from the low field side IN+OUT from both low and high field side LFS_UP LFS_UPPERfrom low field side top UNKNOWN Location of ECRH launch unknown
notes: No string launch-location label in summary. Closest DD field for numeric deposition location: summary/heating_current_drive/ec(0)/position (rho_tor_norm, 'position of maximum ECRH power deposition'). If ECHLOC is a string label (e.g. 'midplane', 'HFS'), no summary field exists → temporary. ec_launchers/launcher(0)/name could hold a string label (machine description IDS).

csv_description: ECRH power in watts absorbed by the plasma. Zero if no ECRH is applied.
notes: Difference between "absorbed" and "coupled"??

csv_description: ICRH heating scheme. Possible Values: NONE If no ICRH power has been applied to discharge OFF If PICRH = 0 but PICRH > 0 at some other time in discharge HMIN for H minority HE3MIN for 3He minority H2NDHARMfor 2nd harmonic H heating HE3MIN-T 3He minority in Tritium HMIN-T H minority in Tritium HHFW UNKNOWN Heating scheme not supplied
notes: No heating-scheme label in summary. Candidate: summary/heating_current_drive/ic(0)/harmonic (integer, 'harmonic number of absorbed ICRH waves') captures part of the scheme. Full scheme string (e.g. 'H minority', 'D-3He') has no DD field. If encoding as harmonic integer, use ic(0)/harmonic as mapped_caveat; for the full label → temporary.

csv_description: ICRH power in watts absorbed by the plasma. Zero, if no ICRH is applied.
notes: Absorbed vs. coupled?

csv_description: Time rate of change of the total plasma stored energy as determined from MHD in watts. ASDEX: As for DWDIA. D3D: Spline fit to W then differentiated. JET: As for DWDIA. JFT2M: Simple derivative over ±5 ms of WMHD without correcting for li change.
notes: DD path confirmed: summary/global_quantities/denergy_thermal_dt (W) = 'Time derivative of the thermal plasma energy content'. WMHD includes thermal + fast-particle contribution; DD path covers thermal only. Suggest mapped_caveat.

csv_description: The electron temperature in eV, determined from the Thomson scattering point that is closest to the magnetic axis. ASDEX: Average of the 3 YAG laser channels closest to the equatorial plane. D3D: Thomson scattering point closest to the magnetic axis (< 10 cm).
notes: Electron temperature at magnetic axis from Thomson scattering. Candidate: summary/local/magnetic_axis/t_e (already claimed by TE0). Same physical location, different diagnostic. Suggest mapped_caveat + needs_source='Thomson scattering', or separate temporary entry.

csv_description: Rotational energy in joules. Wrot = 1/2 M V2 where M is the mass of the thermal ions and V is the thermal ion toroidal velocity. V is deduced from carbon toroidal rotation using NCLASS.
notes: Rotational energy 0.5 M V² (thermal ion toroidal rotation). No summary/global_quantities/energy_rotational confirmed. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Total fast ion energy due to NBI in joules estimated from approximate formula. Zero if no NBI. ASDEX: Regression analysis from 176 FREYA runs. D3D: Slowing-down formula using critical energy and beam energy. JET: 0.16x1019xPINJ/NEV for SHOT<=18760; per-energy formula for SHOT>18760. JFT2M/PBXM/PDX: WFPER + WFPAR.
notes: No NBI-specific fast energy field in summary. Summary totals energy_fast_perpendicular and energy_fast_parallel claimed by WFPER/WFPAR. No per-source NBI fast energy exists in summary. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Estimate of fraction of perpendicular fast ion energy compared to total fast ion energy due to NBI. If WFPER and WFPAR available, WFANI = WFPER/(WFPER+WFPAR). Zero if no NBI. ASDEX/D3D/JET: estimated from regression analyses.
notes: No anisotropy-fraction field in DD. Derivable: WFANI = energy_fast_perpendicular / (energy_fast_perpendicular + energy_fast_parallel). Suggest derived.

csv_description: Estimate of the perpendicular fast ion energy content during ICRH heating in Joules. Defined as 4/3 (DWDIA - DWMHD). Zero if no ICRH. JET: Estimated with PION code.
notes: Candidate: summary/heating_current_drive/ic(0)/energy_fast (J) – 'Fast particle energy content driven by IC waves'. Direct semantic match. Note: DD field is total IC fast energy (not split perp/parallel); WFICRH is described as perpendicular only. Accept as mapped_caveat or mapped if the perp component dominates.

csv_description: Estimate of the parallel fast ion energy content due to ICRH heating in Joules. Zero if no ICRH. JET: Estimated with PION code.
notes: DD has no perp/parallel split per heating source; summary/heating_current_drive/ic(0)/energy_fast is total IC fast energy (candidate for WFICRH). No separate parallel IC fast energy field exists. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Total fast ion energy due to ICRH in joules estimated from approximate formula. Zero if no ICRH. JET: = 0.3 x (TE0/103)1.5 x PICRH x (1019/NE0) / 17 J.
notes: ICRH fast ion energy from approximate formula. Candidate: summary/heating_current_drive/ic(0)/energy_fast (total IC fast energy; already noted as candidate for WFICRH). WFICFORM is a formula-derived alternative estimate. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Indicates, if WFICFORM has been used in confinement calculations (=1).
notes: Flag: WFICFORM used in confinement calculations (=1). Database bookkeeping; no DD field. Suggest discard or temporary.

csv_description: Estimate of fraction of perpendicular fast ion energy compared to total fast ion energy due to ICRH heating. Zero if no ICRH.
notes: No IC anisotropy-fraction field in DD. Derivable once WFICRH and WFICRHP are resolved: WFANIIC = WFICRH/(WFICRH+WFICRHP). Suggest derived (or temporary until WFICRH/WFICRHP are settled).

csv_description: Estimated thermal plasma energy content in Joules. Machine-specific definition: ASDEX: WDIA-1.5xWFANIxWFFORM; AUG: WMHD-0.75xWFPER-1.5xWFPAR; CMOD: WTOT-WFICRH; COMPASS: WDIA; D3D: WMHD-WFFORM; JET: WDIA-1.5(WFPER+WFICRH); JFT2M: WDIA/3+2xWMHD/3-WFFORM; JT60U: WKIN; MAST/START: WMHD-WFFORM; NSTX: WKIN; PBXM/PDX: WMHD-0.75xWFPER-1.5xWFPAR; TCV/TUMAN3M/T10: WDIA.
notes: Estimated thermal plasma energy (machine-specific formulas). Candidate: summary/global_quantities/energy_thermal (already mapped to WKIN from kinetic measurements). WTH and WKIN are different estimates of the same quantity. Suggest mapped_caveat + needs_source noting the estimation method, or temporary to avoid collision with WKIN.

csv_description: Estimated total energy confinement time in seconds. Defined as WTOT/PL.
notes: imas_description is ambiguous about thermal vs. total energy for this field… Anyway, both are derived from DB quantities. But PL, WTH are in temporary/discard

csv_description: Rotation frequency of impurities in center of plasma in rad/s. W = Vtor/Rlocal where Rlocal is R at the point of measurement.
notes: Impurity rotation frequency at plasma center (rad/s). DD stores velocity_phi (m/s) per species at magnetic_axis. Candidate: summary/local/magnetic_axis/velocity_phi/<impurity_species> with formula transform ω = v_phi / RGEO. Caveat: species (Carbon/Oxygen/etc.) is machine-specific. Suggest temporary until species is standardized.

csv_description: Rotation frequency of impurities at the half radius in rad/s. W = Vtor/Rlocal where Rlocal is R at the point of measurement.
notes: Impurity rotation frequency at half-radius (rad/s). DD has velocity_phi at magnetic_axis only in summary; no half-radius location. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Rotation frequency of the main plasma species in the center in rad/s. W = Vtor/Rlocal.
notes: Main plasma species rotation frequency at center (rad/s). Candidate: summary/local/magnetic_axis/velocity_tor/deuterium (m/s) with formula ω = v_tor/RGEO. Caveat: frequency vs velocity distinction, and species (D/H) varies. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Rotation frequency of main plasma species at the half radius in rad/s. W = Vtor/Rlocal.
notes: Main plasma rotation frequency at half-radius. No half-radius rotation in summary. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Net fraction of NBI power in the parallel direction. Positive for co-injection, negative for counter-injection. Computed from beam geometry angles relative to the geometric axis.
notes: Net fraction of NBI power in parallel direction: SPIN = (P_co - P_ctr) / P_total. summary/heating_current_drive/power_launched_nbi_co_injected_ratio (already COCTR) = P_co/P_total. SPIN = 2×COCTR - 1 if beams are purely co or counter. Suggest derived (formula: 2×COCTR - 1), or temporary for asymmetric configurations.

csv_description: Torque on the plasma due to neutral beam injection in Newton-meters. Computed summing over beam sources and energy components (full, half, one-third), using beam energy, mass, power and tangency radius.
notes: NBI torque on plasma (N·m). No summary/global_quantities/torque field found. Candidate: nbi/unit(:)/torque or core_sources/source/global_quantities/torque_tor. Not a simple summary scalar. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Volume-integrated torque due to beam (as calculated in TRANSP) in Newton-meters. Equals TORQ if no TRANSP calculation exists.
notes: Volume-integrated beam torque from TRANSP (N·m). Equals TORQ if no TRANSP calculation. Candidate: same as TORQ note above. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Volume-integrated total input torque (beam torque + torque from applied or intrinsic error fields) in Newton-meters. Equals TORQBM if no additional torques to consider.
notes: Total input torque including error-field contributions (N·m). No DD path found. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Toroidal velocity at center of plasma. Definition to come.
notes: Toroidal velocity at plasma center. 'Definition to come' in source PDF. Candidate when defined: summary/local/magnetic_axis/velocity_tor/deuterium (m/s). Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Volume-averaged toroidal velocity. Definition to come.
notes: Volume-averaged toroidal velocity. Candidate: summary/volume_average/velocity_tor if it exists. Suggest temporary.

csv_description: Toroidal velocity of impurities. Definition to come. Set to NONE. Standard dataset flags.
notes: Impurity toroidal velocity. Set to NONE in DB. Candidate: summary/local/magnetic_axis/velocity_phi/<impurity_species> (m/s). Suggest temporary.
